title: cryptocarya myrcioides
intro: Cryptocarya myrcioides, also known as the Tawa tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The Tawa tree has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Tawa tree can be made into teas or infusions, or used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
